title: Gran Sasso di Italia - dagli occhi del Dji Mavic 4 Pro
creator: @valsoiapg
notes: CANDIDATE-SOURCED, INDEPENDENTLY RE-VERIFIED THIS SESSION -- one exemplar added to the mountain/alpine-peak landscape subgenre bucket. A prior agent's candidate JSON proposed this URL; per this task's step 1 requirement, it was NOT trusted verbatim -- it was re-fetched live in this session via two separate WebFetch calls against https://www.instagram.com/reel/DR96xzDjZ2a/ (standard oEmbed is not usable here: Meta's public Instagram oEmbed endpoint requires an authenticated Graph API app token and is not a bare unauthenticated GET like YouTube's, so a direct page WebFetch was used instead, consistent with this pack's precedent of falling back to direct page fetches when a platform's oEmbed is unavailable, e.g. skypixel-landscape.json's YouTube-watch-page-HTML fallback for CC-marking checks). Call 1 confirmed: caption/title reads verbatim 'Gran Sasso di Italia - dagli occhi del Dji Mavic 4 Pro' (matches the candidate's title modulo capitalization of 'Dji'/'DJI'); posting account is 'valsoiapg'; tagged accounts are @dji_italia, @djiglobal, and @dji; no follower count was rendered on the fetched page; and no Creative Commons marking of any kind is present anywhere on the page. Call 2 (targeted at the comment thread) confirmed the drone model 'DJI Mavic 4 Pro' is stated in the caption, and reproduced the same viewer comment the candidate cited near-verbatim: "Video stupendo! Non capisco perche IG li renda scattosi...tipo 24fps" (roughly, 'Great video! I don't understand why IG makes them choppy... like 24fps') -- a remark about Instagram's own playback/transcoding degrading smoothness, not a comment about the editor's cut rhythm or shot length, so it is NOT used as an editorial/pacing signal (consistent with the candidate agent's own conclusion). A supplementary WebSearch ('valsoiapg instagram Gran Sasso drone') this session did not surface a dedicated per-reel writeup, but did independently corroborate that 'valsoiapg' corresponds to Gianmarco Palazzo, an active, named drone pilot/videographer with visible aerial-content output around the Gran Sasso/Abruzzo region on Instagram and Threads (https://www.threads.com/@valsoiapg) -- a weak-to-moderate real-account/genuine-creator signal, not proof of any award or formal showcase placement (none is claimed here; award_or_showcase_provenance is left null accordingly, per this task's instruction not to invent one for reel-style content with no formal award). Both re-verification calls independently reproduce the candidate JSON's title, creator, tagged accounts, and comment-text findings, so this exemplar is retained with the same 'medium' confidence the candidate flagged -- medium because the only corroboration beyond the reel itself is the tagging of DJI's own regional/global accounts (not proof DJI officially featured/reposted it) plus the one identity-consistency signal from the WebSearch, not a documented contest placement or press writeup. license_category is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved': determination method is identical to every other record in this pack -- absence of any explicit Creative Commons marking on the fetched page is treated as insufficient evidence for a permissive license, per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly and explicitly confirmed otherwise). Consequently local_media_path stays null and no video/image file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo. duration is left null: no runtime was ever displayed by either WebFetch call, and per this pack's convention this field is left unset rather than estimated from the reel's known short-form Instagram format. scores (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are scores_provenance: manually_estimated placeholder judgments, calibrated slightly below this pack's documented contest-winning exemplars (e.g. skypixel-landscape.json's 0.78/0.75/0.82, skypixel11_africa_unseen_ellisvanjason.json's 0.8/0.8/0.85): the caption documents a current flagship consumer drone (DJI Mavic 4 Pro, capable of high-resolution/high-bitrate capture) and the reel is tagged to DJI's own regional/global accounts, both weak-to-moderate polish signals, but there is no documented contest placement, no confirmed resolution/bitrate for this specific upload, no follower/engagement count available as a corroborating polish signal, and the one viewer comment found actually complains about perceived choppiness (attributed to Instagram's own re-encoding, not the source footage) rather than praising smoothness outright -- these NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py), since no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the spec's no-bulk-download constraint. composite_score IS a real computation: it was produced this session by calling this repo's own drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.75, exposure=0.72, motion_smoothness=0.76, composition=None) and drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights() via '.venv/bin/python3' in this session. Note this repo's weights.py has since evolved past the 'default-v1' profile the pack's earlier exemplars' notes describe: default_weights() with no explicit version argument now returns 'default-v2' (sharpness=exposure=motion_smoothness=composition=0.25, added for Milestone 2's real composition scoring) rather than 'default-v1' (equal 1/3 on the three non-composition signals, composition weight 0.0). Because composition's score is still None here (this pack's Milestone-1 Capability-1 convention), compute_composite_score's defensive 'skip any signal whose score is None' behavior excludes composition from both the weighted sum and the renormalization denominator regardless of its now-nonzero 0.25 weight -- so the arithmetic result is identical to the older v1 equal-thirds formula: (0.75+0.72+0.76)/3 = 0.7433333333333333 (rounded to 0.7433 here), confirmed by actually running the function this session rather than hand-computed. The composite arithmetic itself is genuine and reproducible even though its three inputs are estimates rather than measurements.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: No genuine editorial/pacing basis found, confirmed independently this session. Live playback was not attempted (source is an Instagram Reel, not a standard public YouTube URL, per this pack's review-method scope). Two separate WebFetch calls against the live Instagram page this session returned only: the caption title ('Gran Sasso di Italia - dagli occhi del Dji Mavic 4 Pro', tagging @dji_italia/@djiglobal/@dji and naming the DJI Mavic 4 Pro), and a comment thread whose only content-adjacent remark is generic praise plus one comment about Instagram's own playback/transcoding ('...perche IG li renda scattosi...tipo 24fps' -- 'why does IG make them choppy...like 24fps'), which is a complaint about Instagram's re-encoding of the upload, not about the editor's cut rhythm, shot length, or transition choices. A WebSearch for the creator/subject this session ('valsoiapg instagram Gran Sasso drone') surfaced only general creator-identity context (Gianmarco Palazzo / valsoiapg as an active aerial-content creator around Gran Sasso/Abruzzo), nothing describing this specific reel's cuts, shot length, transitions, or overall pacing. No field is populated from a guess.
